{"key_event":"Kansas-Nebraska Act and sectional mass mobilization","reasoning":"Civil society grew stronger through railroads, newspapers, immigrant associations, abolitionism, proslavery mobilization, Know-Nothings, and the rise of the Republican Party, even as this mobilization was deeply exclusionary and violent. State capacity expanded westward and gained coercive reach via fugitive-slave enforcement, but its legitimacy and monopoly on violence were undercut by party-system breakdown, Dred Scott backlash, Bleeding Kansas, and the Utah War’s exposed limits.","society_change":0.5,"society_power":8.3,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":6.8}
